steps.title: Identify what the word must mean
steps.content: The passage describes nature as "gorgeous," with moss "enlivened," pine "resplendent," and festoons of carnival. Mr. Bradford is "conscious" of this vivid natural scene. The blank must mean an impressive visual sight or exhibition that a person can be aware of.

steps.title: Substitute each choice and eliminate
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Spectacle': "this gorgeous spectacle of nature" — a spectacle is a visually striking or impressive sight. This fits the vivid, colorful scene perfectly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Reproduction': "this gorgeous reproduction of nature" — a reproduction is a copy or duplicate. The passage describes real nature, not a replica of it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Pretentiousness': "this gorgeous pretentiousness of nature" — pretentiousness means affectation or trying to seem important. Nature is not described as self-important or affected here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Disguise': "this gorgeous disguise of nature" — a disguise is a concealing costume. Nothing in the passage suggests nature is hiding or concealing anything. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
